title: Murine CD8 T Cell Restimulation In Vitro
author: Kristin Anderson
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.spqedmw

steps.title: Obtain spleens to pulse with peptide
steps.instruction: Obtain spleens to pulse with peptide. Record pertinent information regarding donor mice (mouse background, number of spleens, gender, date of birth).

steps.title: Harvest T cells by smashing spleens through a 40um filter
steps.instruction: Harvest T cells by smashing spleens through a 40um filter. Rinse with 10-20 mL of T cell media and transfer to a 15 or 50 mL tube. Spin down @369 xg for 4-5 min at 4C.

steps.title: ACK Lysis with 2 ml/spleen for...
steps.instruction: ACK Lysis with 2 ml/spleen for 2 min at room temperature.

steps.title: Quench ACK lysis with T cell...
steps.instruction: Quench ACK lysis with T cell media (use an equal or greater amount than the ACK volume).

steps.title: Spin down @369 xg for 4-5 min at 4C
steps.instruction: Spin down @369 xg for 4-5 min at 4C. Resuspend in T cell media and split cells into separate 15 mL tubes to irradiate (1 tube per peptide condition; aliquot 1ml of cells / tube.).

steps.title: Rinse cells immediately by filling tube...
steps.instruction: Rinse cells immediately by filling tube with 10-20 mL of T cell media. Spin down at @369 xg for 4-5 min at 4C. Resuspend in 1-2 mL T cell media. The volume will depend on the number of spleens/cells in the tube. The goal is to pulse with excess peptide. Typically 1ug of peptide is sufficient to pulse up to 5 spleens worth of cells.

steps.title: Add appropriate amount of peptide per tube
steps.instruction: Add appropriate amount of peptide per tube. (e.g. 1ug of peptide for 1-5 spleens)

steps.title: Mix well and incubate at 37C...
steps.instruction: Mix well and incubate at 37C for 60 to 90 minutes (can go longer if necessary).

steps.title: Rinse excess peptide out by washing...
steps.instruction: Rinse excess peptide out by washing with 10-12 mL T cell media.

steps.title: Rinse a second time with 10-12 mL T cell media
steps.instruction: Rinse a second time with 10-12 mL T cell media.

steps.title: Spin down T cells @369 xg...
steps.instruction: Spin down T cells @369 xg for 4-5 min at 4C. Remove supernatant and resuspend in 10 mL T cell media. Count T cells.

steps.title: Combine APCs and T cells at...
steps.instruction: Combine APCs and T cells at a 10:1 ratio (you can also do up to a 1:1 ratio if you don’t have enough APCs, but we have found the 10:1 APC:T cell ratio yields the best T cell expansion). Add T cell media to bring to 10 mL final volume in T25 flasks. Add 50 IU/mL IL-

steps.title: Ideally, plate 1-2e6 T cells and...
steps.instruction: Ideally, plate 1-2e6 T cells and 10-20e6 APCs in an upright T25 flask. For larger T cell preparations, scale up in larger cell culture flasks proportionally.

1. If the flasks are starting to change from orange to yellow in color, add a small volume of media to the flask (up to 10mL in a T25). Do not over-dilute the cells - this will slow their growth and can result in cell death. It if okay to let cells grow in orange media. 2. Add 50 IU/mL of IL-2.
